chapters.title: Greetings & Introductions
chapters.description: Learn essential Italian greetings and how to introduce yourself.

lessons.prompt: What does 'Ciao' mean?
lessons.explanation: 'Ciao' is used both as hello and goodbye informally. For formal: 'Buongiorno' (hello), 'ArrivederLa' (goodbye).

lessons.prompt: Mi ___ Marco. (My name is Marco.)
lessons.explanation: 'Mi chiamo' = my name is. Literally 'I call myself'. From 'chiamarsi' (reflexive verb).

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'Where are you from?'
lessons.hint: 'Di dove' = from where. 'Sei' = are (informal tu form of essere).

lessons.prompt: Which phrase means 'Pleased to meet you'?
lessons.explanation: 'Piacere' = pleasure/nice to meet you. Can also say 'piacere di conoscerti' (nice to meet you, informal).

lessons.prompt: Sono ___ India. (I am from India.)
lessons.explanation: 'Sono dell'India' = I am from India. 'Di + l' = dell'. Or simply: 'Vengo dall'India' (I come from India).

chapters.title: Italian Articles & Nouns
chapters.description: Master Italian definite and indefinite articles and noun genders.

lessons.prompt: Which definite article is used for masculine singular nouns starting with a consonant?
lessons.explanation: 'Il' = masculine singular (consonant). 'Lo' = masc. sing. (s+consonant, z, ps, gn). 'La' = feminine. 'L'' = before vowels.

lessons.prompt: ___ amica è simpatica. (The friend (f) is nice.)
lessons.explanation: 'Amica' starts with a vowel → use 'l'' (not 'la'). L'amica = the (female) friend.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'A red apple'
How do most feminine nouns end in Italian?
lessons.explanation: Most feminine nouns end in -a (la ragazza, la casa) and masculine in -o (il ragazzo, il libro). Exceptions exist.

lessons.prompt: Ho un ___ rosso. (I have a red book.)
lessons.explanation: 'Libro' = book (masculine, -o ending). 'Ho' = I have (avere). Un libro rosso.

chapters.title: Food & Italian Cuisine
chapters.description: Order food, talk about Italian dishes, and navigate a ristorante.

lessons.prompt: What is 'il conto' in a restaurant context?
lessons.explanation: 'Il conto, per favore' = the bill, please. Essential phrase for dining out in Italy.

lessons.prompt: Vorrei ___ caffè, per favore. (I would like a coffee, please.)
lessons.explanation: 'Vorrei' = I would like (polite). 'Un caffè' = a coffee. In Italy, 'caffè' usually means espresso.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'What do you recommend?'
lessons.hint: 'Cosa' = what. 'Consigliare' = to recommend. 'Consiglia' = you recommend (formal Lei).

lessons.prompt: What is 'il primo piatto'?
lessons.explanation: Italian meals: antipasto → primo (pasta/risotto) → secondo (meat/fish) → dolce (dessert).

lessons.prompt: Sono ___ al latte. (I am allergic to milk.)
lessons.explanation: 'Essere allergico a' = to be allergic to. Use 'allergica' if feminine.

chapters.title: Present Tense Verbs
chapters.description: Conjugate regular Italian verbs in the present tense.

lessons.prompt: What is the 'io' form of 'parlare' (to speak)?
lessons.explanation: -ARE verbs present tense: io -o, tu -i, lui/lei -a, noi -iamo, voi -ate, loro -ano.

lessons.prompt: Noi ___ in Italia. (We live in Italy.)
lessons.explanation: 'Vivere' is -ERE verb. Noi form = -iamo: viviamo. Io vivo, tu vivi, lui vive, noi viviamo.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'They finish work at six'
lessons.correctAnswer: Loro finiscono il lavoro alle sei
lessons.hint: 'Finire' is irregular -IRE verb (isc type): finisco, finisci, finisce, finiamo, finite, finiscono.

lessons.prompt: Which is the correct 'tu' form of 'avere' (to have)?
lessons.explanation: Avere (irregular): io ho, tu hai, lui/lei ha, noi abbiamo, voi avete, loro hanno.

lessons.prompt: Lei ___ molto stanca. (She is very tired.)
lessons.explanation: Essere (to be): io sono, tu sei, lui/lei è, noi siamo, voi siete, loro sono. 'È' = she/he is.

chapters.title: Getting Around Italy
chapters.description: Navigate Italian cities, ask for directions, and use public transport.

lessons.prompt: What does 'Dov'è la stazione?' mean?
lessons.explanation: 'Dov'è' = where is (dove + è). 'La stazione' = the station. Essential travel phrase.

lessons.prompt: Vada sempre ___. (Go straight ahead — formal.)
lessons.explanation: 'Dritto' = straight. 'Vada' = imperative of andare (formal). 'Sempre dritto' = always straight.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'Turn right at the traffic light'
lessons.hint: 'Girare' = to turn. 'A destra' = to the right. 'Al semaforo' = at the traffic light.

lessons.prompt: What is 'un biglietto di andata e ritorno'?
lessons.explanation: 'Andata' = going, 'ritorno' = return. 'Solo andata' = one way. 'Andata e ritorno' = return.

lessons.prompt: A che ora ___ il prossimo treno? (What time does the next train leave?)
lessons.explanation: 'Partire' = to leave/depart. 'Parte' = he/she/it leaves. 'A che ora' = at what time.
